preamble: The nasalized open front unrounded vowel /ã/ can undergo a process of weakening, leading to its transformation into the voiced labio-velar approximant /w/. This shift involves a significant change in articulatory features, moving from a nasalized open front vowel to a non-nasal, rounded, labio-velar approximant.
phoneticEffects: This phonetic transformation entails denasalization, backing of the vowel's articulation, rounding of the lips, and a reduction in sonority as the vowel becomes a consonantal approximant

examples.note: Specific examples are documented in the cited source, illustrating the weakening of the nasalized vowel to a labio-velar approximant.

sources: Brøndum-Nielsen, G. (1968). Gammeldansk Grammatik, II (3rd edn), 152–56 (§309.2).
